name: system-integration-validator
description: Validates system integration before deployment. Use when checking ports, database connections, frontend-backend APIs, or debugging blocked/stuck workflows. Detects dead ends, bottlenecks, circular dependencies.
capabilities: Check port availability with lsof commands
Verify PostgreSQL and Redis database connections
Detect dead ends, orphan inputs, and bottlenecks in data flows
Analyze circular dependencies in system architecture
Provide troubleshooting guidance for integration issues
author: BarisSozen
securitySummary: Pure documentation-only skill providing validation guidance. No executable code, network operations, file access, or external commands. Static findings are false positives from misidentified documentation patterns and JSON field names.
valueStatement: Integration failures cause deployment delays and production incidents. This skill provides step-by-step validation for ports, databases, APIs, and data flows to catch issues early.
limitations: Does not execute commands - provides guidance only
Requires user to run validation commands manually
Does not persist validation results between sessions

useCases.title: Pre-deployment validation
useCases.description: Run comprehensive port, database, and API checks before deploying to staging or production environments.

useCases.title: Debug stuck workflows
useCases.description: Identify circular dependencies and data flow issues that cause workflows to hang or fail silently.

useCases.title: Verify API contracts
useCases.description: Ensure frontend and backend API endpoints match to prevent integration bugs.

promptTemplates.title: Basic port check
promptTemplates.prompt: Check if ports 3000, 3001, 5432, 6379, and 8080 are available on my local machine.

promptTemplates.title: Database connections
promptTemplates.prompt: Verify PostgreSQL and Redis connections are working on localhost. Show the commands to test each.

promptTemplates.title: API contract validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me validate that my frontend API calls match my backend endpoints. What should I check?

promptTemplates.title: Data flow analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze my system for dead ends, orphan inputs, bottlenecks, and circular dependencies in the data flow.

bestPractices: Run port checks before starting any services to avoid conflicts
Test database connections with actual query or ping commands, not just socket tests
Use timeouts on all network operations to prevent hangs
antiPatterns: Skipping port checks and discovering conflicts at deployment time
Assuming database connection success means the database is fully functional
Ignoring data flow issues until users report broken workflows

faq.question: Which tools and platforms are supported?
faq.answer: Works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code on any operating system with standard networking tools installed.

faq.question: What are the limits of this skill?
faq.answer: It provides validation guidance only. You must run the actual commands yourself on your system.

faq.question: How do I integrate this with my CI/CD pipeline?
faq.answer: Use the validation steps as a template for your pipeline scripts. The skill outlines the commands to run.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill only provides documentation and guidance. No data leaves your system.

faq.question: What if validation fails?
faq.answer: The skill provides troubleshooting guidance. Common fixes include freeing ports, starting services, or fixing API mismatches.

faq.question: How is this different from other testing tools?
faq.answer: This skill focuses on integration readiness before running tests. It catches environment issues early.
